global_methodology_rules.rule: Standard cost = SUM(component.standard_price × BOM_qty) + SUM(workcenter.costs_hour × operation_duration). Never mix standard and actual costs in the same calculation.
global_methodology_rules.never: Never use actual consumption quantities in standard cost calculations. Never use standard prices in actual cost calculations.

global_methodology_rules.rule: Actual cost = SUM(stock.move price_unit × quantity for consumed components) + SUM(workorder.actual_duration × workcenter.costs_hour). Include scrap valuations in actual cost.
global_methodology_rules.never: Never exclude scrap from actual cost. Never use planned duration for actual cost.

global_methodology_rules.rule: When DuraCube custom fields exist (std_cost, mo_cost on mrp.production), use these as primary source. Fall back to BOM rollup calculation when custom fields are empty or zero.
global_methodology_rules.never: Never ignore pre-calculated custom cost fields when they contain valid data.

global_methodology_rules.rule: Phantom/Kit BOMs must be auto-exploded to their sub-components — never treat a phantom as a leaf node. Stop recursion only at purchased items (no child BOM) or service items.
global_methodology_rules.never: Never return phantom BOMs as leaf components. Never recurse into a BOM already visited (circular detection).

global_methodology_rules.rule: BOM quantities must be scaled to the manufacturing order quantity. If BOM is for 1 unit and MO is for 10, multiply all component quantities by 10 (bomQtyRatio = MO_qty / BOM_qty).
global_methodology_rules.never: Never present BOM quantities without scaling to the requested production quantity.

global_methodology_rules.rule: If a component has its own child BOM (child_bom_id field or found via product_tmpl_id search), recursively calculate its cost from sub-components instead of using its standard_price directly.
global_methodology_rules.never: Never use standard_price for a manufactured component that has a BOM — always recurse.

global_methodology_rules.rule: Total Variance = Actual Cost - Standard Cost. Positive = unfavorable (over budget). Negative = favorable (under budget). This sign convention must be consistent throughout all reports.
global_methodology_rules.never: Never flip the sign convention. Never report favorable variance as positive.

global_methodology_rules.rule: Material Price Variance = (Actual Unit Price - Standard Unit Price) × Actual Quantity. Material Quantity Variance = (Actual Quantity - Standard Quantity) × Standard Unit Price.
global_methodology_rules.never: Never use actual price in quantity variance formula. Never use standard quantity in price variance formula.

global_methodology_rules.rule: Labor Rate Variance = (Actual Rate - Standard Rate) × Actual Hours. Labor Efficiency Variance = (Actual Hours - Standard Hours) × Standard Rate.
global_methodology_rules.never: Never mix rate and efficiency components in variance calculations.

global_methodology_rules.rule: Scrap impact must be calculated per GOOD unit produced: scrap_cost / good_units_produced. Scrap rate = scrap_qty / (good_qty + scrap_qty). Good units = total produced - scrapped.
global_methodology_rules.never: Never divide scrap cost by total units (good + scrap). The denominator for per-unit impact is GOOD units only.

global_methodology_rules.rule: Flag standard_price = 0 as DATA QUALITY WARNING — do not calculate with zero prices. Flag BOM quantity = 0 as DATA ERROR — do not divide by zero. If MO has no work orders, use simplified material-only costing and note the limitation.
global_methodology_rules.never: Never silently accept zero prices as valid. Never fail silently when work orders are missing — degrade gracefully with a warning.

global_methodology_rules.rule: If stock.move has no price_unit or price_unit = 0, flag as 'unvalued movement' and fall back to product.standard_price. Draft and cancelled MOs should be excluded from trend calculations unless explicitly requested.
global_methodology_rules.never: Never include draft/cancelled MOs in cost trend analysis by default.

global_methodology_rules.rule: All monetary values in company base currency (AUD). Round unit costs to 2 decimal places for display. Use 4 decimal places for intermediate calculations to avoid rounding errors.
global_methodology_rules.never: Never display more than 2 decimal places in final output.

global_methodology_rules.rule: WIP (Work In Progress) Manufacturing Orders that are not yet completed should show estimated completion cost based on current consumption, flagged clearly as ESTIMATE — not final.
global_methodology_rules.never: Never present WIP costs as final actual costs without an ESTIMATE flag.

global_methodology_rules.rule: Never modify Odoo data. All operations are READ-ONLY via XML-RPC search_read, read, and read_group methods only.
global_methodology_rules.never: Never call create, write, or unlink methods on any Odoo model.

recommended_tool_chain: 1. Start with search_manufacturing_orders to find the MO
2. Use get_mo_detail for full MO record with work orders and stock moves
3. Use get_bom_structure for hierarchical BOM tree with costs
5. Use get_actual_mo_cost for realized cost (completed MOs only)
6. Use analyze_cost_variance for the variance dashboard
7. Use get_material_variance or get_labor_variance for drill-down
workflow_guidance.aggregation_first: When analyzing multiple MOs, use get_cost_trends or compare_mo_performance instead of calling get_actual_mo_cost repeatedly. Aggregation tools are more context-efficient.
workflow_guidance.estimation_workflow: For pre-production analysis, use estimate_production_cost (no MO needed) or what_if_analysis for scenario modeling.
workflow_guidance.knowledge_first: Call get_cost_methodology on first interaction to understand the domain rules, formulas, and terminology before analyzing data.

summary.BOM: Bill of Materials — the recipe for manufacturing a product
summary.MO: Manufacturing Order — an order to produce a specific quantity
summary.WO: Work Order — a task within an MO assigned to a work center
summary.WC: Work Center — a machine or station where work happens
summary.Variance: The difference between what it should cost and what it actually cost
summary.Favorable: Actual cost was LESS than expected — good news
summary.Unfavorable: Actual cost was MORE than expected — needs attention

detailed.BOM: Bill of Materials — hierarchical recipe defining components and operations needed to manufacture a product. Can be multi-level (sub-assemblies within assemblies).
detailed.Phantom BOM: A virtual BOM that auto-explodes into its components. Never appears as a manufactured item — always breaks down to its parts.
detailed.Standard Cost: Pre-determined cost calculated from BOM component prices + work center hourly rates × operation durations. Used as the baseline for variance analysis.
detailed.Actual Cost: Realized cost from actual stock move valuations (what was really consumed) + actual work order durations × work center rates.
detailed.Price Variance: (Actual Price - Standard Price) × Actual Qty — measures whether you paid more or less per unit than expected.
detailed.Quantity Variance: (Actual Qty - Standard Qty) × Standard Price — measures whether you used more or less material than planned.
detailed.Rate Variance: (Actual Rate - Standard Rate) × Actual Hours — measures whether labor cost more or less per hour than standard.
detailed.Efficiency Variance: (Actual Hours - Standard Hours) × Standard Rate — measures whether work took more or less time than planned.
detailed.Scrap Rate: scrap_qty / (good_qty + scrap_qty) — percentage of total output that was scrapped.
detailed.WIP: Work In Progress — MO started but not yet completed. Costs are estimates until MO is done.

technical.mrp.production: Odoo model for Manufacturing Orders. Key cost fields: std_cost, mo_cost, l_cost_diff, m_cost_diff.
technical.mrp.bom: Odoo model for Bill of Materials. Types: normal (standard), phantom (auto-explode), subcontract.
technical.mrp.bom.line: Odoo model for BOM component lines. Links to product_id with quantity and optional child_bom_id for recursion.
technical.mrp.workorder: Odoo model for Work Orders. duration_expected vs duration gives efficiency variance.
technical.mrp.workcenter: Odoo model for Work Centers. costs_hour is total; labour_cost_per_hour and wc_cost_per_hour split labor vs overhead.
technical.stock.move: Odoo model for inventory movements. raw_material_production_id links consumed materials to MO. price_unit gives actual unit cost.
technical.stock.scrap: Odoo model for scrap records. production_id links to MO.
technical.stock.valuation.layer: Odoo cost tracking layer. unit_cost and value fields for precise valuation.
technical.GL Mapping: Standard cost → Cost of Goods Manufactured. Variance → Manufacturing Variance account. WIP → Work in Progress account. Scrap → Scrap/Waste account.
